memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir Nicholas Haute
memoryArtifacts.description:
Sir Nicholas Haute (Hawte), MP (Became owner of Igtham Mote, High Sheriff of Kent and Keeper of Canterbury Castle) also named Hathaway (Shakespeare's Gloucestershire Connections)

zh.Bishopsbourne, Kent, England: 英國肯特比曉普斯布恩
zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Haute於1357年9月20日出生在英國肯特沃爾坦時，他的父親Edmund Haute11354歲，母親Benedicta De Shelving18歲。他於大約1389年與Alice Coven結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1416年1月13日在英國英格蘭艾可易亞斯特 霍登享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Haute于1357年9月20日出生在英国肯特沃爾坦时，他的父亲Edmund Haute11354岁，母亲Benedicta De Shelving18岁。他于大约1389年与Alice Coven结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1416年1月13日在英国英格兰艾可易亞斯特 霍登享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: Haute
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname or status name from an argot word (thieves’ slang) in Bavaria and Austria for a peasant farmer (see   Hotz   1). Compare   Houts   and   Houtz  .

variants.name: Hotz
meanings: South German and Swiss German: from thieves' cant for a peasant or farmer, which in turn derives from an ancient Germanic personal name, Hugizo or Huzo, pet forms of a compound name formed with hug- as the first element (see, for example, Hubert , Hugh ).
South German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German hotzen ‘to run swiftly’.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Haute was born on 20 September 1357, in Waltham, Kent, England, his father, Edmund Haute, was 11354 and his mother, Benedicta De Shelving, was 18. He married Alice Coven about 1389. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 13 January 1416, in East Horndon, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Haute est né le 20 septembre 1357, à Waltham, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Haute, avait 11354 ans et sa mère, Benedicta De Shelving, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Alice Coven environ 1389. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 13 janvier 1416, à East Horndon, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Haute(이)가 1357년 9월 20일 영국 켄트 월섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Haute(은)는 11354세였고, 그의 어머니 Benedicta De Shelving(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1389년 Alice Coven(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1416년 1월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 이스트 호른던에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.
